title: salix repens var. angustifolia
intro: Salix repens var. angustifolia, also known as narrow-leaved creeping willow, is a variety of creeping willow, a type of deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the willow family. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Salix repens var. angustifolia include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is typically prepared as a tincture or infused in tea, and it can also be used to make salves and ointments for topical application.
